results.possibleIdentity: Lewis MacAdams (1944–2020) was an American poet, journalist, filmmaker, and environmental activist who was active in the avant-garde art and literary scenes starting in the mid-1960s. He is widely recognized for his work with the 'New York School' of poets and his later performative activism in California. MacAdams was involved with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), notably contributing to 'The New York Collection for Stockholm Portfolio' (1973), a major collaborative project organized by E.A.T. to benefit the Moderna Museet in Stockholm. His early career included the publication of poetry collections such as 'City Money' (1966) and 'The Poetry Room' (1971). In the 1970s, he moved to Bolinas, California, and later served as the director of the Poetry Center at San Francisco State University from 1975 to 1978. MacAdams is perhaps best known for co-founding the 'Friends of the Los Angeles River' (FoLAR) in 1985/1986, which he described as a '40-year art work.' This project began with a performative act of cutting a hole in the river's chain-link fence to advocate for its restoration.
